2603.28698.title: EpiScreen: Early Epilepsy Detection from Electronic Health Records with Large Language Models
2603.28698.paper_summary: Epilepsy and psychogenic non-epileptic seizures often present with similar seizure-like manifestations but require fundamentally different management strategies. Misdiagnosis is common and can lead to prolonged diagnostic delays, unnecessary treatments, and substantial patient morbidity. Although prolonged video-electroencephalography is the diagnostic gold standard, its high cost and limited accessibility hinder timely diagnosis. Here, we developed a low-cost, effective approach, EpiScreen, for early epilepsy detection by utilizing routinely collected clinical notes from electronic health records. Through fine-tuning large language models on labeled notes, EpiScreen achieved an AUC of up to 0.875 on the MIMIC-IV dataset and 0.980 on a private cohort of the University of Minnesota. In a clinician-AI collaboration setting, EpiScreen-assisted neurologists outperformed unaided experts by up to 10.9%. Overall, this study demonstrates that EpiScreen supports early epilepsy detection, facilitating timely and cost-effective screening that may reduce diagnostic delays and avoid unnecessary interventions, particularly in resource-limited regions.
2603.28698.paper_summary_zh: 癲癇和心理性非癲癇發作通常表現出類似的發作樣徵，但需要根本不同的管理策略。誤診很常見，可能導致診斷延遲、無謂的治療以及患者的重大病痛。儘管長時間的視頻腦電圖是診斷的金標準，但其高昂的成本和有限的可及性妨礙了及時診斷。在這裡，我們開發了一種低成本且有效的方法EpiScreen，通過利用電子健康記錄中常規收集的臨床筆記來進行早期癲癇檢測。通過對標記筆記進行大規模語言模型的微調，EpiScreen在MIMIC-IV數據集上達到了高達0.875的AUC，在明尼蘇達大學的私有隊列上達到了0.980。在臨床醫生與AI的合作環境中，EpiScreen輔助的神經科醫生的表現比未受輔助的專家高出多達10.9%。總的來說，本研究表明EpiScreen支持早期癲癇檢測，促進及時且具成本效益的篩查，可能減少診斷延遲並避免不必要的干預，特別是在資源有限的地區。
2603.28698.author: Shuang Zhou et.al.
2603.28698.authors: Shuang Zhou, Kai Yu, Zaifu Zhan, Huixue Zhou, Min Zeng, Feng Xie, Zhiyi Sha, Rui Zhang
